items.title: Drop Python 3.9 support, require Python 3.10+
items.body:
Raises the minimum supported Python version to 3.10 across the project metadata and CI/CD workflows.

## Changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added `requires-python = ">=3.10"`
- **`ci.yml`**: Removed `"3.9"` from the test matrix
- **`cd.yml`**: Pinned `python-version` from `3.x` to `3.10`

items.title: Drop Python 3.9, add Python 3.14 support
items.body:
This updates the repository’s declared/tested Python support to include 3.14 and aligns the surrounding tooling/docs metadata with that support level. It also adjusts request tests for Python 3.14’s event-loop behavior without changing runtime code.

- **Packaging and support metadata**
  - Add the `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14` classifier in `pyproject.toml`
  - Extend `.python-version` to include `3.14` so multi-version checks exercise it

- **Docs and tooling alignment**
  - Move docs-related Python pins from `3.13` to `3.14` in:
    - `.readthedocs.yml`
    - `scripts/docs`
    - `scripts/serve`

- **Request test compatibility on 3.14**
  - Keep `pybotters/request.py` unchanged
  - Update `tests/test_request.py` to pass an explicit mock loop when constructing standalone `ClientRequest` instances outside an active event loop

```python
req = ClientRequest(
    method,
    url,
    params=None,
    headers=None,
    data=data,
    auth=None,
    loop=Mock(),
)
```

## Changes proposed in this pull request:

- Updates to the latest godojo release and python version
- Updates uwsgi command to use new location of the wsgi file

## Things to check

- For any logging statements, is there any chance that they could be logging sensitive data?
- Are log statements using a logging library with a logging level set? Setting a logging level means that log statements "below" that level will not be written to the output. For example, if the logging level is set to `INFO` and debugging statements are written with `log.debug` or similar, then they won't be written to the otput, which can prevent unintentional leaks of sensitive data.

feat(python): Python SDK alpha (v0.1.0a1)
items.body:
Ships the Python surface alongside the TypeScript compiler. Same shared IR contract, same Swift output, two host languages.

## What's new

**python/axintai/**
- `ir.py` — Python mirror of the TS IR (cross-language contract)
- `parser.py` — Python DSL parser for @intent-decorated functions
- `sdk.py` — High-level \`defineIntent()\` API, mirrors the TS surface
- `cli.py` — \`python -m axintai compile <file>\` command
- \`__init__.py\` — package exports

**python/tests/** — 17 tests, all green (parser + SDK)

**python/examples/create_event.py** — 28-line quickstart

**python/pyproject.toml** — published on PyPI as \`axintai\` v0.1.0a1 (Python 3.11+, Apache 2.0)

## Why

The moat isn't the compiler — it's \`one definition → every agent surface\`. Python unlocks the huge ML/AI developer community that's more likely to ship Siri integrations than write Swift.

## Scope

- Zero changes to \`src/\` (TypeScript compiler unchanged)
- Self-contained under \`python/\`
- Does not block the other feature PRs

## Tests

\`\`\`
cd python && python -m pytest
# 17 passed
\`\`\`

## Follow-ups (tracked in ROADMAP v0.3.0)

- Formal Intent Manifest spec at spec.axint.ai so TS and Python parsers share a single grammar contract
- Expand Python test suite from 17 → parity with TS (currently 117 TS tests)
- `axintai` → `@axintai/python` namespace alignment post-v1.0

items.title: Fix `ChangeType` `UnsupportedOperationException` on JS `FunctionCall` nodes
items.body:
## Motivation

When running Java recipes (e.g., `NoGuavaPredicate` → `ChangeType`) against repositories containing JavaScript files, `ChangeType.postVisit()` throws `UnsupportedOperationException` on `JS.FunctionCall` nodes. This was discovered in flagship recipe run alerts on app.moderne.io — the "Java best practices" recipe run against Netflix+Spring org produced 46 per-file errors on `metaflow/plugins/cards/ui/prism.js`.

The root cause: `ChangeType.postVisit()` has explicit handling for `J.MethodInvocation` and `J.NewClass` (both `MethodCall` implementations), but `JS.FunctionCall` — also a `MethodCall` — falls through to the generic `TypedTree` branch, which calls `withType()`. All `MethodCall` implementations throw `UnsupportedOperationException` on `withType()` because the type is derived from the method type.

Additionally, `J.ClassDeclaration.type` (a `JavaType.FullyQualified`) was not being serialized over RPC, causing JavaScript class declarations to lose their type attribution when crossing the RPC boundary.

## Summary

- Add a `MethodCall` catch-all branch in `ChangeType.postVisit()` between the `J.NewClass` and `TypedTree` branches, using `withMethodType()` instead of `withType()`
- Add `J.ClassDeclaration.type` to the RPC sender/receiver in all language implementations (Java, TypeScript, C#, Python)
- Add `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest` in `rewrite-javascript` that verifies `ChangeType` can handle JS source containing `FunctionCall` nodes

## Test plan

- [x] New `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest.functionCallDoesNotThrow()` — applies `ChangeType("Foo", "Bar", false)` to JavaScript containing a class with a method returning a function, verifying both the class rename and that `JS.FunctionCall` nodes don't cause exceptions
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeTest` in `rewrite-java-test` passes
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest` in `rewrite-groovy` passes
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeTest` in `rewrite-kotlin` passes

Thank you for contributing to NVIDIA DALI! If you haven't yet,
please read the contributing guidelines in the CONTRIBUTING.md file.

## Category:

**Other** (*e.g. Documentation, Tests, Configuration*)


## Description:

The DALI backend Pybind11 extension already declares support for free-threaded Python. This PR does the same for `python_function_plugin`. This is the missing piece for full free-threaded Python support.

items.body:
Three backend dependencies had known CVEs. Updated to patched versions in `backend/requirements.txt`.

## Changes

- **Pillow** `10.3.0` → `12.1.1` — out-of-bounds write when loading PSD images (affects `>= 10.3.0, < 12.1.1`)
- **python-jose** `3.3.0` → `3.4.0` — algorithm confusion vulnerability with OpenSSH ECDSA keys
- **python-multipart** `0.0.9` → `0.0.22` — arbitrary file write via non-default config + DoS via malformed `multipart/form-data` boundary

- api-change:`mediaconnect`: \[`botocore`] Adds support for MediaLive Channel-type Router Inputs.
- api-change:`redshift-data`: \[`botocore`] The BatchExecuteStatement API now supports named SQL parameters, enabling secure batch queries with parameterized values. This enhancement helps prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities and improves query reusability.

- api-change:`endpoint-rules`: \[`botocore`] Update endpoint-rules client to latest version
- api-change:`ivs-realtime`: \[`botocore`] Adds support for Amazon IVS real-time streaming redundant ingest.

- api-change:`outposts`: \[`botocore`] Add AWS Outposts APIs to view renewal pricing options and submit renewal requests for Outpost contracts

- api-change:`braket`: \[`botocore`] Added support for t3, g6, and g6e instance types for Hybrid Jobs.
- api-change:`connect`: \[`botocore`] The voice enhancement mode used by the agent can now be viewed on the contact record via the DescribeContact api.
- api-change:`datasync`: \[`botocore`] Allow IAM role ARNs with IAM Paths for "SecretAccessRoleArn" field in "CustomSecretConfig"
- api-change:`datazone`: \[`botocore`] Update Configurations and registerS3AccessGrantLocation as public attributes for cfn
- api-change:`ec2`: \[`botocore`] EC2 Capacity Manager adds new dimensions for grouping and filtering capacity metrics, including tag-based dimensions and Account Name.
- api-change:`ecs`: \[`botocore`] This release provides the functionality of mounting Amazon S3 Files to Amazon ECS tasks by adding support for the new S3FilesVolumeConfiguration parameter in ECS RegisterTaskDefinition API.

- api-change:`endpoint-rules`: \[`botocore`] Update endpoint-rules client to latest version
- api-change:`lambda`: \[`botocore`] Launching Lambda integration with S3 Files as a new file system configuration.

It provides fast, direct access to all of your S3 data as files with full file system semantics and low-latency performance, without your data ever leaving S3.

items.title: refactor: consolidate duplicate Python helpers into pkg/tools/python/
items.body:
## What Changed

Five Python venv/pip orchestration helpers duplicated between the hook executor (python_executor.go) and the framework service ( ramework_service_python.go) are now shared through pkg/tools/python/. Both consumers already depended on this package, so no new dependency edges were introduced. This eliminates ~80-100 lines of duplication and ensures future changes only need to happen in one place.

## How It Works

Extracted shared logic into exported functions/methods on python.Cli: VenvNameForDir() for naming, EnsureVirtualEnv() for stat→create, InstallDependencies() for pip/pyproject dispatch, ResolveCommand() for py/python/python3 resolution, and VenvPythonPath()/VenvActivateCmd() for platform-aware venv paths. The executor's stricter implementation (with rrors.Is and directory type checks) was used as the canonical version. The pythonTools interface was simplified accordingly.

## Issue References

Fixes #7570
Relates to #7435

items.title: [Python] Only use Python Limited C API in ROOT Pythonizations library
items.body:
This follows up on 4e0c7bf0dda25984c, swapping out the remaining use of CPython API functions that were not inside the limited API.

In the cases where we're sill using private headers from cppyy, the parts of these headers that don't compile with the limited API are excluded via the `Py_LIMITED_API` preprocessor macro.
